analysis_result: 29 samples are located in the spinal region (Location == 'Spinal').
denominator_used: N = 108; definition: all rows in the 'Clinical Data' sheet of 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx with non-null Tumor_ID.
assumptions_made: Filtered to rows with non-null Tumor_ID per data notes (all 108 rows have non-null Tumor_ID); Location values counted via exact string match to 'Spinal' (case-sensitive); no other filtering applied.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - File: 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx, sheet 'Clinical Data'
   - Shape: 108 rows x 15 columns
   - Relevant columns: Tumor_ID, Location

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - Filter 1: Tumor_ID non-null -> 108 remaining (all rows satisfy)
   - Final analytic cohort: N = 108

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Location column value_counts (dropna=False):
     Peripheral    46
     Cutaneous     33
     Spinal        29
   - No NaN values present in Location column.

D. COMPUTATION
   - Numerator: count of rows where Location == 'Spinal' (exact, case-sensitive match)
   - Denominator: 108 samples with non-null Tumor_ID
   - Computation: (df_filt['Location'] == 'Spinal').sum() = 29

E. RESULT
   - 29 samples have Location == 'Spinal' (29/108 = 26.9%).

F. CAVEATS
   - Counts are straightforward descriptive statistics; no statistical inference applied.
   - Location column had no missing values, so no imputation or missing-data handling was required.
